title: Kalman-Style Latent Wear and Innovation Features
summary: A recent F1-specific state-space paper models tire performance as a latent process with pit-stop resets and interpretable degradation dynamics.
rationale: A recent F1-specific state-space paper models tire performance as a latent process with pit-stop resets and interpretable degradation dynamics. This is a concrete upgrade over generic latent tyre-state ideas: filtered state, slope, innovation, and uncertainty may be more stable than raw `LapTime_Delta` and `Cumulative_Degradation`.
implementation_hint: Within each `Driver x Race x Stint`, fit a simple linear-Gaussian filter or lightweight state-space approximation using lap-time-derived observations and reset on pit stops; export filtered wear level, acceleration, one-step residual, and posterior variance as tabular features.
expected_effect: Can denoise noisy lap observations and sharpen pre-stop hazard around genuine degradation cliffs.
risk: Traffic, yellow-flag effects, and outlier laps can contaminate the observation model if the filter is too naive.
